commit.message:
fix(scripts): resolve filelist paths to absolute before xrun invocation

xrun resolves paths inside -f filelists relative to CWD, not relative
to the filelist location. All three filelists (MPTDC, TOP, VIP) use
paths relative to their own directory, causing FILEMIS errors when
xrun is invoked from a different directory.

Add resolve_flist.sh helper that expands relative-path filelists into
absolute-path temp files. All runner scripts now generate these before
invoking xrun.

commit.message:
Replace Semver NuGet package with internal SmolSemVer implementation (#16033)

* Replace Semver NuGet package with internal SmolSemVer implementation

The Semver 3.0.0 NuGet package depends on Microsoft.Extensions.Primitives
5.0.1 which has an expired author certificate, breaking NuGet signature
verification in CI deployment tests (TypeScript AppHost scenarios).

This replaces the external Semver dependency with SmolSemVer, a minimal
zero-dependency SemVer 2.0.0 implementation in src/Shared/SmolSemVer.cs
that uses Span<T> for allocation-free parsing.

Changes:
- Add src/Shared/SmolSemVer.cs with SemVersion type, SemVersionStyles enum,
  and expressive comparison methods (IsNewerThan, IsOlderThan, IsAtLeast,
  HasSamePrecedenceAs)
- Add tests/Aspire.SmolSemVer.Tests with 182 tests including compatibility
  validation against the original Semver package
- Remove Semver PackageReference from Aspire.Hosting and Aspire.Cli
- Replace all using Semver with SmolSemVer across 44 files
- Replace raw int comparisons with expressive methods at all call sites

Fixes #15222

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- Add Aspire.SmolSemVer.Tests to Aspire.slnx
- Fix remarks: 'allocation-free' -> 'minimize allocations'

activation check inside backtrack_and_retry (resolver/mod.rs L391).

backtrack iteration hits the existing-pin check, finds the pin still
doesn't satisfy ^1.1, and continues — exhausting all choices and
returning Err(Conflict).

Input validation:
- Tensor device (CUDA not CPU), contiguity warnings

Error propagation:

perf(bench): reset Maglev deopt cooldown after warmup + track deopt categories

After 100-iteration warmup, benchmarks like sieve_primes accumulate
7 deopts with exponential cooldown (next_try_at=133+). Criterion's
short measurement window (10 samples × 1s) never clears the cooldown,
so Maglev tried=0 executed=0 during measurement.

Fix: call ba.reset_maglev_deopt_count() after warmup for all 9
precompiled benchmarks. This ensures Maglev re-enters on the first
Criterion iteration.

Also: populate MAGLEV_DIAG_DEOPT_CATS counters (were declared but
never incremented) so CI logs show which deopt category fires.

commit.message:
Remove Razor ProjectSystem.SDK refs

Microsoft.VisualStudio.LanguageServices.Razor already checks in its generated ProjectSystem rule files for Core MSBuild, so the merged Roslyn tree does not need Razor's local Microsoft.VisualStudio.ProjectSystem.SDK package or its pinned 17.14.143 version. Removing those Razor-specific entries lets restore defer to Roslyn's existing ProjectSystem packages and avoids the NU1109 downgrade.

commit.message:
fix(slugs): prevent crawler merge from overwriting translated slugs

Three fixes for slug stability across all 94+ crawlers:

Bug A (root cause): mergeLocaleTextMap without sourceLocale used
"longer wins" heuristic, letting English-derived slugs (with hash
suffixes) overwrite properly translated locale slugs. Changed to
"existing wins" — b only fills gaps, never overwrites a.
hardenJobLocaleFields still propagates fresh base titles to the
source locale, so title updates are not blocked.

Bug B: regenerate-slugs-by-locale.mjs updated slugByLocale.it
without syncing job.slug (master). The stale master caused
cleanPreviousSlugsPerLocale to incorrectly remove the old IT
slug from previousSlugsByLocale. Now also updates job.slug when
the IT slug changes.

Data fix: Recovered TSMG job master slug — synced to translated
IT slug and captured old English-derived slug in previousSlugs.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
